steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This Cross-Text Connections question asks how Text 1 and Text 2 relate. The decisive clues: Text 1 reports Lin et al.'s surprising eDNA finding and then presents Price and Ellison's hypothesis to explain it. Text 2 identifies a fatal flaw in that hypothesis — surface remains would be destroyed by scavengers and weathering in under a thousand years, far less than the several millennia the hypothesis requires.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Text 1 discusses two approaches to studying mammoth extinction without advocating for either, whereas Text 2 advocates for one approach over the other.": Text 1 does not weigh two competing research approaches; it presents one study's findings and one explanatory hypothesis. Text 2 does not advocate for an approach at all — it refutes the hypothesis. ✗
  • "Text 1 presents findings by Lin and colleagues and gives another research team's attempt to explain those findings, whereas Text 2 provides additional detail that calls that explanation into question.": Matches exactly: Text 1 = Lin et al.'s eDNA findings + Price/Ellison preservation hypothesis; Text 2 = scavengers and weathering undermine the preservation hypothesis. ✓
  • "Text 1 describes Lin and colleagues' study and a critique of their methodology, whereas Text 2 offers additional details showing that methodology to be sound.": Text 1 contains no methodological critique; it presents a positive hypothesis. Text 2 does not defend Lin et al.'s methods; it attacks the Price/Ellison hypothesis. ✗
  • "Text 1 argues that new research has undermined the standard view of when mammoths went extinct, whereas Text 2 suggests a way to reconcile the standard view with that new research.": Both the "undermining" finding and the attempted reconciliation appear in Text 1. Text 2 does the opposite of reconciling — it shows the reconciliation attempt fails. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
